methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
OpenStar, a Seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Ratu Mataira and Thomas Berry, has raised $35M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers in Climate & Energy alongside 1 public comparable (LNZA), including prominent private companies such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach. Using the $35M raised as a proxy for enterprise value baseline and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, this method implies a valuation of $25.0B. A Peer-Median approach was not applicable due to insufficient narrow-filter comparables within Climate & Energy at the same stage or adjacent stages; the broader peer set lacks the minimum three same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints required for meaningful median analysis.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $21.2B (low) to $28.7B (high), with a median point of $25.0B. The analysis is constrained by reliance on a single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier for this specific stage transition, the representativeness of the $35M raised figure as a valuation proxy, and the absence of direct peer valuation data at comparable stages. Additional market data or transaction comps would strengthen conviction in this range.
